name: haircut-matrix-demo
description:
Self-contained, no-validator demo of the SSR risk-tier model. Walks every recognised asset class (TOKENIZED_DEPOSIT, STABLECOIN, SOVEREIGN_BOND, CORPORATE_BOND, FUND_UNIT, COMMODITY, EQUITY, REAL_ASSET) and renders the default haircut alongside the haircut-adjusted collateral value at three sample notional sizes (10K, 100K, 1M). Useful for asking: how much credit does an equity-class position actually get under cross-margin? Which classes are 'near-cash' (0% haircut)? Where does the heaviest risk-tier discount land?

Runs in milliseconds against pure-Rust `ssr-types` constants — no Solana validator, no LiteSVM, no deployed program. Pairs naturally with `compliance-gate-demo` (one surfaces the transfer-permission model, the other the collateral-credit model).
headline: 8 asset classes ordered safest → riskiest (0% → 40% haircut); EQUITY collateral credits at 70¢ on the dollar, REAL_ASSET at 60¢.

steps.explanation: Enumerate every recognised SSR asset class via `ssr-types::default_haircut_bps` and project haircut-adjusted collateral at three sample notional sizes.

expected_outcomes.name: two-zero-haircut
expected_outcomes.description: At least 2 classes credit at full notional (TOKENIZED_DEPOSIT + STABLECOIN)

expected_outcomes.name: monotonic-by-risk
expected_outcomes.description: Haircuts are monotonically non-decreasing in the safest-first ordering

expected_outcomes.name: real-asset-forty
expected_outcomes.description: REAL_ASSET defaults to 4000 bps (40%) haircut — heaviest standing

expected_outcomes.name: heaviest-at-least-40-pct
expected_outcomes.description: The riskiest tier carries at least a 40% haircut
